Chapter II of "Hancock Brothers of Virginia" published in 1992 by Maureen Hancock Ward follows.

The ancestry of Benjamin Hancock, father of William and Stephen has been debated by researchers for yea …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Benjamin Franklin Hancock於1710年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素可時，他的父親John Hancock37歲，母親Jane Holt40歲。他於1732年與Elizabeth Jameson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1762年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州古倫德享年52歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Benjamin Franklin Hancock于1710年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素可时，他的父亲John Hancock37岁，母亲Jane Holt40岁。他于1732年与Elizabeth Jameson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州劍橋大學默里 肯特结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1762年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州古倫德享年52岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州亨利可。

nameMeanings.name: Hancock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hann + the hypocoristic suffix -cok, which was commonly added to personal names (see Cocke ).
Dutch: from Middle Dutch hanecoc ‘winkle, periwinkle’ (a type of shellfish), probably a metonymic occupational name for someone who gathered and sold shellfish.

History: Thomas Hancock, the uncle of Declaration of Independence signatory John Hancock (1736/7–93), was among the foremost of 18th-century American businessmen. He was a descendant of Nathaniel Hancock, who was known to have been in Cambridge, MA, as early as 1634. Born in Braintree, MA, John Hancock was president of the Second Continental Congress and the first governor of the state of MA.

variants.name: Hancox
meanings: English: variant of Hancock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Handcock
meanings: English: variant of Hancock with intrusive -d-.

variants.name: Hann
meanings: German and Dutch: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Johann (see John ).

English: from the Middle English personal name Hann, commonly a pet form of Henry (Middle English Hanry). Hann could also be a rhyming pet form of Randolph (Middle English Randal, shortened to Ran or Rand). Han was also a pet form of Joh(a)n in Middle Dutch, and it is possible that this usage was brought to England by Flemings after the Norman Conquest, but no evidence has yet been found that it was similarly used in Middle English. See Henry and Randall , and compare Hankin , Hancock , and Hanks .
English: variant of Ann with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Benjamin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the youngest of the twelve sons of Jacob. His mother Rachel died in giving birth to him, and in her last moments she named him Benoni, meaning ‘son of my sorrow’. His father, however, did not wish him to bear such an ill-omened name, and renamed him Benyamin (Genesis 35:16–18; 42:4). This means either ‘son of the right hand’ or more likely ‘son of the south’ (Hebrew yamin can also mean ‘south’), since Benjamin was the only child of Jacob born in Canaan and not in Mesopotamia to the north. Another tradition is that the second element of the name is a variant of the Hebrew plural noun yamim, which means ‘days’ but is used idiomatically to mean ‘year’ or ‘years’. The name would then mean ‘son of (my) old age’ and refer to the fact that Benjamin was Jacob's youngest child. In the Middle Ages the name was often given to sons whose mothers had died in childbirth. Today it has no such unfortunate associations and it grew enormously in popularity following the release of the film The Graduate ( 1967 ), in which Dustin Hoffman played the role of Benjamin Braddock . It is used in Scotland as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Beathan .

nameMeanings.name: Franklin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman’, denoting a member of a class of men who were not of noble birth but who were nevertheless freeholders. The vocabulary word is derived from Old French franc, meaning both ‘free’ and ‘Frankish’. The connection between freemen and Franks is reflected in the Late Latin term francalia, originally denoting lands held by Franks, which came to mean lands not subject to taxes. The given name is now quite common, especially in the United States, where it is often bestowed in honour of the statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin ( 1706–90 ). A more recent influence was President Franklin D. Roosevelt ( 1882–1945 ).

variants.name: Franklyn
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman’, denoting a member of a class of men who were not of noble birth but who were nevertheless freeholders. The vocabulary word is derived from Old French franc, meaning both ‘free’ and ‘Frankish’. The connection between freemen and Franks is reflected in the Late Latin term francalia, originally denoting lands held by Franks, which came to mean lands not subject to taxes. The given name is now quite common, especially in the United States, where it is often bestowed in honour of the statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin ( 1706–90 ). A more recent influence was President Franklin D. Roosevelt ( 1882–1945 ).

historicEvents.title: Mount Vernon
historicEvents.description: Mount Vernon Plantation was the home of George Washington. It started off as 2,000 acres and was later expanded to 8,000 acres. The house itself started off as a six room building then got extended to twenty-one rooms.

en.lifeSummary: When Benjamin Franklin Hancock was born in 1710, in Sussex, Virginia, British Colonial America, his father, John Hancock, was 37 and his mother, Jane Holt, was 40. He married Elizabeth Jameson in 1732, in New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1762, in Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 52, and was buried in Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Benjamin Franklin Hancock est né en 1710, à Sussex, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, John Hancock, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Jane Holt, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Jameson en 1732, à New Kent, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1762, à Goochland, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Henrico, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

ko.lifeSummary: Benjamin Franklin Hancock(이)가 1710년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hancock(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Holt(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1732년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 뉴 켄트에서 Elizabeth Jameson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1762년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 구클랜드에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 헨리코에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Benjamin Franklin Hancock nasceu em 1710, em Sussex, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, John Hancock, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Jane Holt, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Jameson em 1732, em New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1762, em Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial Ame: ню кэнт, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.Albemarle Parish, Surry, Virginia, Briti: альбемарл пэриш, сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Benjamin Franklin Hancock родился в 1710 г. в местности суссекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени John Hancock было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Holt, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Jameson в 1732 г. в местности ню кэнт, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1762 г. в местности гучланд, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности хенрику, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Benjamin Franklin Hancock nació en 1710, en Sussex, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, John Hancock, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Jane Holt, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Jameson en 1732, en New Kent, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1762, en Goochland, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Henrico, Virginia, British Colonial America.
